summary: The Strait of Hormuz closure has sent an energy shock through global markets and supply chains. February PPI surged 0.7% month-over-month — the largest jump in over two years — pushing the year-over-year rate to 3.4%. The narrative has shifted from "when will the Fed cut" to "will it cut at all," with markets having earlier priced two cuts this year now pricing none.
tweet: Hormuz closure and a hot PPI print have markets repricing Fed cuts entirely, with fear dominating gold, bonds, and stocks.
